name: Linear_Issue_Tracker
description: Manages issues and projects in Linear as the Mind's external execution layer. Creates, organizes, prioritizes, and tracks issues. Bridges cognitive intent from the Mind's WORK surface to actionable items in Linear project management system.
progressive_unlock: Issue reading and team listing available immediately. Issue creation and updates unlock after Mind has retrieved existing issues at least once to understand the workspace structure.
constraints: Never expose Linear API token in logs or outputs
Respect Linear rate limit of 1500 requests per hour with complexity-based calculation
Do not create or update issues without explicit Steward confirmation unless the Mind has standing permission in TENET
Sync issue state with WORK surface — Linear issues should mirror WORK board cards where applicable
Do not create duplicate issues — always check existing issues first via Linear_ListIssues
Issue identifiers follow TEAM-NUMBER format (e.g., ENG-123); validate format before API calls
Store active team IDs and project IDs in STREAM.entity for quick access
All API requests use GraphQL POST to https://api.linear.app/graphql with Bearer token in Authorization header

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed. Verify priority values are 0-4, issue identifiers match TEAM-NUMBER format.
Retrieve Linear API token from TENET.apiKey.linear. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'.
Determine intent: review (list issues/teams/projects), read (get issue details), create (new issue), or update (modify existing issue).
For team/project discovery: execute Linear_ListTeams to populate available teams. Fetch Linear_ListProjects for active projects. Store team IDs and project IDs in STREAM.entity for reuse.

For issue review: fetch Linear_ListIssues with appropriate filters (teamId, state, assigneeId, priority). For daily review, filter by state 'Todo' or 'In Progress'. Cross-reference with WORK surface cards for consistency.
For single issue details: execute Linear_GetIssue with the issue identifier (TEAM-NUMBER format). Retrieve full issue metadata for decision-making.

For issue creation: check existing issues first via Linear_ListIssues to prevent duplicates. Construct GraphQL mutation via Linear_CreateIssue with required teamId and title. Optionally set priority, assignee, labels, and project. Obtain Steward confirmation before proceeding. If issue maps to a WORK card, store the Linear issue ID in the card's data field.

For updates: fetch current issue state first via Linear_GetIssue, apply requested changes via Linear_UpdateIssue. Obtain Steward confirmation before state or priority changes. If WORK card exists for this issue, update its status accordingly.

Compile issue summary with team names, issue identifiers, state, priority, assignees, and target dates. Push daily issue summary to LTM with key: 'linear_daily_{date}'. Update STREAM with active issue counts by team and state.
